133472.explanation: Accessibility of the city center must be made as easy as possible for seniors. Public transport does not always function optimally and is sometimes not available on certain days.

133474.explanation: The need for care exceeds such requirements. It also does not promote mutual understanding for others.

133478.explanation: Also in English and in pictograms.

133479.explanation: Permanent jobs are important, but a flexible layer is necessary.

133480.explanation: Fewer rules stimulate initiative and foster connection within society.

133481.explanation: Given the existing housing shortage, this is a no-go.

133483.explanation: 2035 is not realistic. Eindhoven Airport must also revise nitrogen targets given the presence of larger aircraft.

133484.explanation: Aspiring to good and healthy living can never be discouraged.

133486.explanation: See our answer to statement 33. Innovation must be encouraged.

133487.explanation: Let's first cover all roofs of houses and business premises before considering landscape pollution.

133488.explanation: But the municipality does not contribute financially.

133490.explanation: Already existing through the Leergeld Foundation.

133492.explanation: Safety and everything we can deploy to make life safe has priority. And the old adage applies here too: those who have nothing to hide have nothing to fear. Nowadays, you can hardly walk through any street without being caught on cameras on houses and garages.

133493.explanation: The rule also applies here: everything that contributes to the feeling of safety is welcomed enthusiastically by 50Plus.

133494.explanation: Avoid illegal street vending and also avoid monopolization by a single coffeeshop.

133496.explanation: Addresses the housing shortage and the mixing of young and old leads to mutual understanding and looking out for each other.

133497.explanation: Fewer long commutes between home and work. Friends and family live in the same place. Increases social cohesion.

133498.explanation: Although there are already agencies where complaints can be filed, it often appears that not enough is done for the tenant.

133499.explanation: There are already forms of good cooperation between the two cities from the MRE and SGE. Collaborating in a housing corporation that provides mid-range rent can only create win-win situations.

133500.explanation: Also business and retail premises. See Belgium.

133501.explanation: Each city has its own specific requirements and needs. Therefore, there must remain room for local rules.

133502.explanation: Stimulates turnover. Any stimulation in this regard is welcome.

133503.explanation: 130% was already in our previous program and is again included in the new election program.

133504.explanation: But it must apply to all neighborhoods. Poverty occurs in all strata and residential environments.

133505.explanation: But provided that accessibility is well arranged at all times.

133507.explanation: It is a matter of safety. On some ring roads within the neighborhoods such as the Rijpelbaan (Rijpelberg district) and Rivierensingel (Brouwhuis district), driving is sometimes far too fast.

133508.explanation: There are already options to reach the center by public transport for little money. Free public transport would also become unaffordable.

133511.explanation: Current generations cannot be held accountable for the actions of distant ancestors. What would Queen Maxima have to do now?

133452.explanation: Residents of Helmond must be able to participate in decision-making. The referendum may even be binding in our view! 'Participation' is rightly perceived as one-way traffic from the municipality. Also, a 'citizens' council' does not go far enough. Residents therefore do not feel taken seriously. A referendum is democracy.

133454.explanation: For many people, a dog is more than a pet; it is a 'family member', a companion. A dog provides social warmth, increases the feeling of safety, and reduces loneliness. No taxes are also paid for other pets. We support tax reduction for everyone.

133455.explanation: Economically it may seem positive if it, for example, creates more employment for Helmond residents. But due to the pressure on the electricity network, it is not feasible. Some companies feel forced to move as a result. Make existing industrial estates more profitable.

133456.explanation: Residents must be able to decide for themselves which energy source they want to use, without any pressure from the municipality. They decide how much money they want to spend on it; this should not be paid from public funds. Also, switching off gas or buying, for example, a heat pump, should not be mandatory.

133457.explanation: Many starters and young families in Helmond have been waiting for appropriate housing for years. Naturally, they have the first right to a home. The lack of housing is partly due to the enormous influx of immigrants and refugees. First house our own people, then look further.

133458.explanation: Integration is the own responsibility of everyone who comes to live in Helmond. This also applies to the costs. If integration proceeds slowly, this should not lead to extra costs for Helmond taxpayers. Those who cannot integrate would better choose remigration.

133459.explanation: It is primarily the employer's responsibility. This prevents extra pressure on Helmond's housing market. There are already too few homes and seasonal workers do not intend to stay permanently in Helmond. Housing must be of good quality and safe.

133460.explanation: Disposing of waste must be easy and free. This prevents illegal dumping and 'additional deposits', which cause annoyance, insecurity, (odor) nuisance, and vermin. The more people can dispose of waste for free, the less the municipality has to collect. Therefore, lower waste taxes!

133461.explanation: Every Helmond resident should be allowed to choose how their house is heated. If it is done with an open fireplace or wood stove, energy costs can also be reduced. Additionally, it adds more atmosphere to the home. No extra rules from the municipality that spoil living enjoyment.

133462.explanation: Well-maintained grass and other greenery is a calling card for our city. It promotes livability and provides more space for playing and other recreation. Along bike paths and roads, it also positively influences traffic safety. Preserve as much natural greenery as possible.

133463.explanation: Everyone is responsible for disposing of bulky waste at the (preferably free) recycling center. It must be prevented that this remains on the street or ends up elsewhere in the neighborhood. Pickup leads to extra costs, while municipal charges should decrease.

133464.explanation: Exercise is important, but mainly also an individual responsibility. This can also be done without membership, in public spaces. Make municipal sports parks available to people who want to exercise or move individually or in small groups, without financial obligations.

133466.explanation: A maximum of 30% may be social rental housing. To achieve sufficient diversity in the housing supply, there must be a focus on more affordable mid-range rental homes and affordable owner-occupied homes. This stimulates the housing market and creates opportunities for starters and young families in Helmond.

133467.explanation: There is a need for more housing in Helmond, also for people who cannot afford an entire home. Property owners should have the freedom to rent out single rooms or part of a house. Exploitation must be prevented, but without extra rules from the municipality.

133468.explanation: As much socially useful work as possible should be done. This gets people out of social isolation and can help them develop towards possible paid employment. They can thus show what they (still) can do, instead of what they can no longer do.

133469.explanation: Municipal taxes are too high. They must be lowered; but for all Helmond residents. FVD supports general tax reduction, at the expense of the number of municipal employees, expensive contractors, and regulations. In the case of exemptions, the community pays. That is not fair.

133470.explanation: Because the healthcare system falls short, informal care is unfortunately necessary. A compensation for this is reasonable. In cases where full-time informal care is not required, it is still desirable to apply for jobs to encourage participation in the labor process.

133472.explanation: Helmond’s city center must remain easily accessible for residents, visitors, and suppliers of our entrepreneurs. As long as no reasonable alternative exists in the city, such as well-organized and affordable public transport, the car remains a necessary means of transport.

133473.explanation: An entrepreneur can get into financial trouble due to government measures (Corona!) or unprofessional business practices. In the latter case, the community should not bear the costs. That is personal responsibility. Practical help from fellow entrepreneurs is more desirable.

133474.explanation: In general, being able to choose your own care is important, due to the many options. But not based on religion, culture, or beliefs. Care must be equal for everyone, without preferential treatments or exclusions. This also applies to care providers.

133477.explanation: The municipality must be transparent to its residents about everything it does. This applies especially to automated actions that may have financial consequences for residents. These algorithms should also serve to prevent mistakes (human errors).

133478.explanation: Learning the Dutch language is a prerequisite for good integration and is the responsibility of the individual. There are sufficient opportunities to learn Dutch. Communicating in Dutch challenges people to master the language faster.

133479.explanation: The company that can carry out the assignment best should receive the contract. That company must have the freedom to decide which employees to use. This way, self-employed persons also have a fair chance of being hired.

133480.explanation: Due to (additional) municipal regulations, organizing events is becoming increasingly difficult. Procedures must be shorter and applications should be simpler. Initiatives must be rewarded and traditions should be preserved. This promotes the atmosphere and livability in Helmond.

133481.explanation: FVD wants less, but preferably no shelter for asylum seekers. This group increases pressure on healthcare, housing, and public order & safety. Additionally, residents often experience frequent and significant nuisance from this group. Many other municipalities already understand this and therefore do not accept asylum seeker centers or expansions.

133483.explanation: Being 'climate neutral' is an unrealistic political goal that consumes a lot of expensive taxpayer money, which disappears into a bottomless pit. Human influence on the climate is virtually nil. Therefore, it’s not about a specific year. Climate adaptation, on the other hand, sounds credible.

133484.explanation: 'Nature-inclusive building' is, according to FVD, just another extra rule arising from what we see as flawed climate policy. Construction should be able to happen with fewer rules and more freedom for builders and citizens.

133485.explanation: This is not a task for the municipality. Such meeting places often lead to nuisance, vandalism, and/or pollution. This costs the community a lot of money repeatedly. The perpetrators are responsible for their behavior and its consequences. Deviant behavior should not be rewarded.

133486.explanation: Many charging stations are already unused and other cars are not allowed to park there. The popularity of electric driving is clearly declining. The electricity network in the Netherlands is increasingly overloaded, with outages as a real risk. Electric driving will never become the norm.

133487.explanation: The 'energy transition' is not a necessity but a political choice that costs us a lot of money. 'Solar fields' pollute the cityscape and come at the expense of valuable nature and agricultural land that our farmers and growers need to provide us with their quality products.

133488.explanation: Basically, everyone should be free to be who they want. FVD opposes putting people in 'boxes'. Focusing on a specific target group has the opposite effect and polarizes. The municipality should radiate neutrality in every way; at events, in publications, and even at its physical locations.

133490.explanation: Music lessons are already given in schools. This is also important for the development of our children. FVD supports a general tax reduction for all Helmond residents, so everyone can afford more and set their own priorities, such as extra music lessons.

133491.explanation: This is not a task or responsibility of the municipality. The municipality can facilitate internships by reducing regulations. Businesses and schools in Helmond are already collaborating well on this. Internship places should be equally available for all relevant students.

133492.explanation: Everyone in Helmond has a right to their privacy and should not constantly feel 'watched'. Furthermore, facial recognition software still has many flaws, leading to errors that can victimize innocent Helmond residents.

133493.explanation: Increasingly, residents of Helmond have to turn to a BOA (special enforcement officer) for 'minor issues' instead of the police, who have other priorities. Good accessibility is an important condition to address a problem that exists now, immediately. Delay often leads to frustration for both parties.

133494.explanation: There is a 'market' for a second coffeeshop in Helmond. It is important to carefully choose the location to prevent nuisance and insecurity. A second coffeeshop should also not attract people from outside Helmond.

133496.explanation: Due to an inadequate healthcare system, demand seems to be increasing for this type of housing where an entire family lives together. However, it should not be a norm for every plan from 100 homes onwards, as each additional rule can cause construction delays. This can also deter parties.

133497.explanation: Many starters and young families in Helmond have been waiting for suitable housing for years. Naturally, they have the first right to a home! The housing shortage is partly due to the enormous immigration and refugee influx. First house our own people, then look further.

133498.explanation: Responsibility for handling complaints lies with housing corporations/landlords; not with the municipality. The municipality should point out shortcomings to housing corporations/landlords. A separate team requires extra capacity and thus community funds. FVD wants a smaller government.

133499.explanation: Helmond must manage its own affairs and does not need Eindhoven for that. A 'regional' housing corporation will never focus fully on Helmond, meaning Helmond home seekers may have less chance of finding suitable housing in their own city.

133500.explanation: It is unfortunate when a house stands empty for a long time, but the ownership rights of the homeowner must remain protected. An owner may decide what to do with their property. No extra taxes or levies and no coercion from the municipality. So also no mandatory housing.

133501.explanation: No jungle of restrictive rules. No extra rules for sustainability or purchasing heat pumps and no obligation to disconnect from gas. No extra costs for participation in district heating projects.

133502.explanation: Seniors should not feel forced to leave their homes because the housing market is frozen, partly due to massive immigration. The community should not bear the moving costs; it remains a personal choice. Others do not receive relocation compensation either.

133503.explanation: This is just 'putting band-aids' on, due to excessive charges. FVD wants a general tax reduction for all Helmond residents, paid for by scaling down the overly expensive municipal organization. Currently, the municipality has one employee per approximately 94 residents; that is absurd.

133504.explanation: Financial problems occur not only in the 'poorest' neighborhoods. FVD wants a general tax reduction for all Helmond residents, funded by reducing the overly expensive municipal organization. Setting up neighborhood desks requires additional (costly) capacity, which must again be paid from community funds.

133505.explanation: The Kasteel-Traverse is a lifeline for Helmond in many ways. Through traffic (especially heavy) should be diverted, but this road must remain optimally accessible for business/social car traffic, deliveries, and emergency services. More exits actually help reduce speed, making it safer.

133507.explanation: Motorized traffic will take longer routes, which can lead to delays and more pollution. It can never become a general norm in a city like Helmond. It only becomes discussible if there is demonstrable danger in residential areas or around schools or sports facilities.

133508.explanation: Public transport is arranged provincially and is indeed expensive. To promote public transport, it should be cheaper for everyone. Creating distinctions polarizes, which is undesirable. Moreover, the community should not bear the cost.

133511.explanation: Our history must be assessed in the context of the era in which events took place. We should learn from mistakes, but history cannot be changed. Apologizing for something you did not do, to someone who did not personally experience the injustice, is pointless.

133452.explanation: DENK Helmond wants inclusive decision-making for all residents. Referenda can cause polarization and exclusion. We prefer broad participation via dialogue, platforms, and neighborhood meetings so that minorities are also heard.

133454.explanation: DENK Helmond opposes abolishing the dog tax. The revenue helps finance facilities like dog parks, waste bins, and enforcement. If this tax disappears, costs will have to be passed on to other residents.

133455.explanation: DENK Helmond opposes more business parks. We choose efficient use and sustainability of existing locations. Expansion must not come at the expense of green spaces, living environments, and housing space, and must align with local needs.

133456.explanation: DENK Helmond supports residents who generate sustainable energy together. This helps both the climate and wallets. The municipality should actively and financially support this, so sustainability remains affordable and not only reachable for the wealthy.

133457.explanation: Housing is a right. Reserving social rental housing for status holders is legally and morally obligatory and promotes housing, integration, and equal opportunities, while the municipality must ensure sufficient housing for everyone.

133458.explanation: Good integration requires investment. DENK wants newcomers to quickly access language education, work, and social activities so they can participate fully and feel connected to society.

133459.explanation: Those who let people work must also ensure good housing. This prevents abuses and protects both labor migrants and local residents.

133460.explanation: Accepting waste for free at the recycling center encourages responsible waste management and prevents illegal dumping. This promotes a clean and healthy living environment for everyone.

133461.explanation: A ban on open fireplaces and wood stoves reduces air pollution and health complaints, especially among vulnerable groups. Restrictions are necessary to protect everyone.

133462.explanation: More frequent mowing of public grass contributes to a neat and safe outdoor space without compromising biodiversity. This increases livability in neighborhoods.

133463.explanation: Free pick-up prevents litter on the streets and ensures tidy neighborhoods, which is good for livability, health, and safety.

133464.explanation: For children, it is essential that financial barriers do not hinder participation in sports. For adults, the municipality should mainly promote a healthy lifestyle in other ways, for example through accessible neighborhood initiatives, exercise programs, and public sports facilities. This makes sports accessible to everyone without the municipality having to fully reimburse sports club memberships.

133466.explanation: The housing shortage mainly affects people with low incomes. DENK Helmond aims for at least 40% social rental housing in new construction, but a minimum of 35% is a step in the right direction and aligns with the goal to keep housing affordable and accessible for everyone.

133467.explanation: Renting out individual rooms can actually contribute to affordable housing for students and singles. Banning it reduces freedom of choice and housing options.

133468.explanation: Social work can be valuable, but must never be imposed as coercion or punishment. Guidance and tailored support towards participation are more effective than imposing obligations.

133469.explanation: For many people, exemption from municipal charges is essential to make ends meet. It prevents poverty and debt. Taking away this support would severely impact the most vulnerable and increase inequality and insecurity of existence.

133470.explanation: Informal care is valuable and demanding work. Those caring for a family member should be exempt from job application requirements to continue providing this care. It is humane to take this into account and not place extra pressure on informal caregivers.

133472.explanation: More car-free streets in the city center can reduce accessibility. DENK wants to maintain accessibility for everyone, including the elderly and people with limited mobility.

133473.explanation: Entrepreneurs deserve a second chance. Debt assistance while preserving the business prevents bankruptcies, maintains income, and avoids job losses for the city.

133474.explanation: Care must align with a person's faith, culture, or beliefs. Culturally sensitive care improves quality, trust, and accessibility. DENK Helmond has already submitted a motion to include this as a fixed requirement in municipal care policy.

133477.explanation: Transparency about algorithms is crucial to prevent discrimination and errors. Residents have the right to know how decisions about benefits and minimum policy are made; technology must never lead to discrimination or incomprehensible decisions.

133478.explanation: The municipality should communicate in other languages where necessary to reach all residents and promote inclusion, especially for important information.

133479.explanation: We believe companies with permanent employees should not automatically be given priority for municipal contracts. The municipality should primarily consider quality, social added value, and local involvement rather than just the type of employment contracts.

133480.explanation: Event regulations protect safety and the living environment. Removing rules must not lead to nuisance, exclusion, or reduced accessibility.

133481.explanation: DENK Helmond supports cooperation with an asylum seekers center. This aligns with our values of humane reception, integration, and legal obligations (distribution law).

133483.explanation: Climate neutrality by 2050 allows for a feasible transition that remains affordable and excludes no one.

133484.explanation: Nature-inclusive building is allowed but not as a mandatory requirement that delays construction or makes housing more expensive. Affordability takes precedence.

133485.explanation: More meeting places and activities for young people strengthen social bonds, prevent boredom, and provide opportunities for talent development.

133486.explanation: More charging stations in all neighborhoods make electric driving accessible to everyone and promote sustainable mobility.

133487.explanation: Solar panels in rural areas can make a valuable contribution to the energy transition. Flexibility and careful integration are essential; rigid bans unnecessarily hinder sustainable development.

133488.explanation: The municipality must commit to equality, safety, and acceptance of everyone year-round. This requires structural policy and attention, not participation in a single specific celebration. The municipality’s efforts should be unifying and include all residents, regardless of background, faith, or lifestyle.

133490.explanation: Music lessons for children from low-income families promote talent development, self-confidence, and equal opportunities.

133491.explanation: An internship is an essential part of their education and forms a first step toward their career. MBO students with a migration or refugee background also deserve a fair chance at an internship. Discrimination and exclusion must play no role.

133492.explanation: Facial recognition in public spaces invades privacy and can lead to misuse. We choose safety without mass surveillance.

133493.explanation: Direct contact with enforcement officers every day increases trust, accessibility, and rapid problem-solving in the neighborhood.

133494.explanation: DENK Helmond does not want a second coffee shop; priority lies with prevention and limiting crime, nuisance, and health risks.

133496.explanation: Multi-generational homes promote care within families, social cohesion, and efficient use of housing space.

133497.explanation: Priority for people already living, working, or studying in Helmond hinders equal opportunities and can cause discrimination. Housing allocation must be fair and inclusive so that everyone has equal access regardless of current residence or workplace.

133498.explanation: A tenants' team supports renters with complaints and helps address abuses, improving housing quality.

133499.explanation: Setting up a mid-rent housing corporation together with Eindhoven increases affordable supply and regional cooperation.

133500.explanation: Vacancy taxes encourage owners to rent out or sell homes, contributing to addressing the housing shortage.

133501.explanation: Housing construction must be faster and simpler. Municipal regulations often cause delays and extra costs. Therefore, the municipality should not impose additional rules beyond national regulations, allowing for faster and more affordable building.

133502.explanation: Moving allowances for seniors stimulate housing flow and make smaller, appropriate homes accessible.

133503.explanation: Lowering the income threshold for contributions harms the lowest incomes. Support must be maintained to protect livelihood security.

133504.explanation: Neighborhood desks for financial aid in the poorest areas offer low-threshold support and prevent problems from worsening.

133505.explanation: Fewer lanes on the Kasteel-Traverse may worsen traffic flow. Accessibility and safety must remain priorities.

133507.explanation: 30 km/h speed limits should be applied only where necessary for safety (mainly around primary schools); through roads must remain smooth and accessible.

133508.explanation: Free public transport for low-income people increases mobility, opportunities, and access to work, care, and education.

133511.explanation: More attention to the history of slavery promotes awareness, recognition, and inclusion. Apologies are just a first step.

133452.explanation: Agree. We consider it very important to take residents' opinions into account on important issues. This means involving them as early as possible in proposed municipal plans.

133454.explanation: Disagree. Dog fouling remains a major nuisance! For that reason alone, the dog tax should continue to exist. For many years it has been a so-called earmarked tax, meaning all proceeds are spent on an efficient dog policy.

133455.explanation: Disagree. We believe that first we must redesign and improve existing business parks so more companies can settle there. That means, for example, we will have to build more vertically, also on top of existing buildings, such as parking on roofs.

133456.explanation: Agree. We should support this citizen-initiated initiative. It clearly contributes to a broadly supported sustainable climate policy.

133457.explanation: Disagree. Because we believe that Helmond residents who have often waited many years should have priority. Refugees with a residence permit should join the existing waiting lists in our opinion.

133458.explanation: Disagree. Because we think newcomers should learn Dutch first. Being able to speak and understand Dutch (preferably also reading and writing) is the foundation for integration. These language lessons could be provided, for example, through schools or volunteers.

133459.explanation: Agree. Because it concerns temporary housing and because there is a severe housing shortage, our clear preference is for housing on the companies' own premises.

133460.explanation: Agree. Although the costs are currently relatively low, nowadays a lot of waste is illegally dumped or deposited, including in forests or rural areas, and even in neighborhoods near certain containers or in random places. This likely costs us more money.

133461.explanation: Agree. We realize that we are prohibiting an activity that is enjoyable for (many) residents. But it does cause nuisance, particularly from smoke and odor, and is also (very) harmful to health. Electric BBQs or gas BBQs should still be allowed.

133462.explanation: Agree. From conversations with our residents, we have clearly heard this wish. They also feel this applies to playgrounds and smaller play areas in the neighborhoods.

133463.explanation: Agree. Our proposal is to offer residents the opportunity at least four times a year to have their bulky waste collected at home free of charge. That provides a solution especially for those who do not have a car to go to the recycling center.

133464.explanation: Agree. Everyone should have the opportunity to participate in sports. If the financial means are not available, the municipality should pay for it. Physical activity (also for adults) should be strongly encouraged.

133466.explanation: Agree. That means that of every 100 homes to be built (rental and owner-occupied combined), at least 35 should be social rental homes. This should also apply to all apartment complexes still to be built.

133467.explanation: Disagree. Renting out one (or possibly multiple) room(s) offers many people, often young adults, the chance to start living independently. We do advocate for good regulations to ensure prices remain reasonable.

133468.explanation: Agree. We have supported this for some time. It does not have to be full-time; it could be a certain number of days or half-days. The condition is that people can do it physically. It contributes, among other things, to a daily rhythm, social contact, and some responsibility.

133469.explanation: Disagree. Far too many people depend on this to make ends meet. Stopping it would immediately cause financial problems, lead to debt, less food intake, less or no heating, and so forth.

133470.explanation: Agree. These activities and actions help people fill their days in a very positive way and provide a great service to society. They should certainly receive something in return.

133472.explanation: Agree. Provided residents receive a permit, preferably free, but otherwise at a (strongly) reduced rate. One-way traffic could be considered. This then offers the possibility for a bicycle lane.

133473.explanation: Agree. But only under certain conditions. If an entrepreneur goes bankrupt, it causes vacancies, layoffs, etc. If debt counseling allows the entrepreneur to continue (possibly downsized) and keep (much of) the staff, that is much better.

133474.explanation: We base ourselves on the UN Disability Convention of 2006. This states that inclusivity and accessibility apply to people with and without disabilities. Faith, culture, and beliefs are not included in that.

133477.explanation: Agree. First of all to explain what an algorithm entails. Most residents do not know the term at all. If they understand it is a clear sequence of steps to be taken, it makes the information and procedure much more accessible.

133478.explanation: Agree. Everyone in the Netherlands must be able to speak Dutch. Residents who cannot should then arrange their own interpreter.

133479.explanation: Agree. If it can be arranged and done, we should definitely do it. The interests of employees are very important to us.

133480.explanation: Agree. Events are an important part of the cultural experience in Helmond. They are mostly organized by volunteers or with many volunteers. Otherwise, events would be unaffordable. The rules should help, not hinder.

133481.explanation: Disagree. The municipality already has its hands full with all its existing matters and concerns. Helmond itself already requires more than enough attention. More asylum seekers (or a reception center) definitely do not fit at this time.

133483.explanation: Agree. Nationally, the goal is to be climate neutral by 2050. Helmond should not try to get ahead of that. Apart from the fact that 2035 is not achievable in our opinion, we should not aim for that. More important matters, like building and housing, need priority now.

133484.explanation: Agree. Green roofs, facade gardens, nesting boxes in walls, insect hotels, and permeable paving all contribute significantly. Provided building costs do not become more expensive.

133485.explanation: Agree. But it must be ensured that nuisance is prevented. Good agreements and easy-to-use rules can provide the right basis for that.

133486.explanation: Disagree. If no car is parked, no other (non-electric) car can park there. Parking pressure in the neighborhoods is already too high. Residents should arrange their own charging station. That should be possible with solar panels on your roof.

133487.explanation: Agree. Our rural area is already so small. No solar fields belong there. Besides, we must first use all other options and possibilities, such as solar panels on factory and house roofs, above various parking lots. There are many more examples to think of.

133488.explanation: Disagree. We are not against a possible Pride week. But the municipality should not participate. Like any other event, the Pride week can apply for a permit.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.moreInfo: In Helmond dog owners currently pay a dog tax. The Municipalities Act gives municipalities the option to levy a dog tax. Municipalities may not levy taxes on other animals. A municipality may decide how to use the revenue from the dog tax. The municipality of Helmond uses the proceeds to create and maintain dog-walking fields, but also to clean up dog waste.
statements.pro: Some parties consider the dog tax outdated. Some municipalities have abolished the dog tax. For many residents the dog is a family member and reduces loneliness. The dog tax is unfair; the municipality does not tax other animals.
statements.con: Some parties say the municipality pays for dog facilities from dog tax revenue, for example dog-walking areas, bins and enforcement. Without a dog tax, non-dog owners would have to contribute via municipal taxes to dog facilities. Dog foul causes nuisance and annoyance.

statements.title: Helmond should have more business parks.
statements.moreInfo: The demand for space for businesses is increasing, partly due to economic growth. The Helmond Business Parks Vision 2025 describes four clusters with eleven business parks in Helmond. These account for nearly 21,000 jobs. More business parks can provide extra jobs, but also take up space that could otherwise be used for housing, nature or recreation.
statements.pro: Some parties think new business parks are important because they provide jobs. Business parks drive economic growth. Some companies will move to the new business parks.
statements.con: Some parties think it is wiser to renovate and improve existing business parks. Creating new business parks leaves less space for housing and nature. A new business park is not feasible due to an overloaded power grid.

statements.theme: Priority for people with refugee status
statements.title: The municipality should continue to reserve a portion of social rental homes as they become available, giving priority to refugees with a residence permit.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet een deel van de vrijkomende

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.
The municipality's treasury.
The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.

statements.moreInfo: Every municipality in the Netherlands is given the task each year by the national government to arrange housing for a certain number of refugees with residence permits (status holders). In 2025 Helmond was tasked with housing 149 status holders. In addition, Helmond had a backlog of 36 status holders. In 2025 Helmond housed 153 status holders, mostly in vacated social rental homes.
statements.pro: Some parties point out that housing refugees with a residence permit is a legal task. Each year every municipality is told how many of these refugees they must arrange housing for. Giving priority to refugees with a residence permit ensures reception centers do not become overcrowded.
statements.con: Some parties point out that residents of Helmond will have to wait longer for a social rental home as a result. Giving priority to status holders reduces support for refugee accommodation.

statements.title: Dropping off all waste at the recycling center should be free.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van alle afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.moreInfo: Dropping off waste at the recycling center is partly free for Helmond residents. This applies, for example, to cooking oil, diapers and old paper. For items such as construction and demolition waste, prunings and household waste a fee must be paid. The revenue is used by the municipality to process and remove the waste. If the municipality stops charging for this, it will have to pay the costs in another way. That means the municipality will have to cut other spending or raise taxes.
statements.pro: Some parties say this lowers the threshold for properly disposing of waste. There will then be fewer illegal waste dumps in residential areas and nature.
statements.con: Some parties note that there are costs associated with the recycling center. If residents who bring waste to the recycling center no longer pay, the waste disposal levy will increase. Then all residents will have to pay.

statements.title: The municipality should pay for sports club memberships for adults with low incomes.
statements.moreInfo: Anyone who wants to join a sports club must pay a membership fee. This can be a barrier for adults with a low income. Stichting Leergeld currently pays the membership for children up to and including 18 years old at sports clubs if their parents have a low income. For adults there is no such scheme in Helmond.
statements.pro: According to some parties, sport is important for physical and mental health. Sport should be possible for everyone. Playing sports together is a way to meet others.
statements.con: Some parties argue sport can also be done outside clubs, in a park or forest. Sport is the personal responsibility of adults. It is unnecessary to pay for everything for low-income adults who want to exercise.

statements.title: The municipality should introduce a consultative referendum so that the residents of Helmond can express their opinion on important issues.

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

titleParts.text: invoeren, zodat de inwoners van Helmond hun mening kunnen geven over belangrijke onderwerpen.

statements.moreInfo: In a referendum a government poses a question on a single topic to voters. They can vote for or against. In a municipal referendum all residents aged 18 or older may vote. If the municipal council wants a referendum, this is called a consultative referendum. If citizens want a referendum, this is called a citizen-initiated (advisory) referendum. They must collect signatures for this. The municipal rules state how many signatures are required at minimum. The result of a referendum is not binding. The municipal council can still make a different decision.
statements.pro: Some parties believe a referendum gives residents more influence. A referendum can increase trust in politics. A referendum is democratic.
statements.con: Some parties believe residents should be able to have input in advance, for example via neighborhood meetings. This is better than voting afterwards in a referendum. The municipal council is elected to make decisions on behalf of residents. A referendum leads to more division.

statements.title: More streets in Helmond city center should become low-traffic.

titleParts.text: In de binnenstad van Helmond moeten meer straten

titleParts.glossary: That there is little car traffic.

statements.moreInfo: Cars currently drive through many streets in Helmond city center. If more car-reduced streets are created there will be less or no through-traffic and more space for pedestrians and cyclists. This can make the city center more pleasant and safer. But it can also mean the city center is less accessible and parking becomes more difficult.
statements.pro: Some parties say a low-traffic inner city provides a safer and healthier living environment. There is more space for trees and plants and for meeting one another.
statements.con: Some parties say that a low-traffic inner city makes the center less accessible. In any case, the car will remain the main mode of transport for many people.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
statements.moreInfo: In Helmond residents can take bulky waste to the recycling center or have it collected at home for a fee. Bulky waste consists of large items such as furniture that do not fit in the household bin. Free curbside collection can make it easier to dispose of bulky waste, but it also brings extra costs for the municipality.
statements.pro: Some parties think this will lead to less illegal dumping. This creates cleaner, more livable neighborhoods.
statements.con: Several parties point out that this costs a lot of money. That will lead either to cuts in other expenditures or to a higher waste disposal charge.

statements.title: People who need care should, as much as possible, be able to choose a care provider who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten zoveel mogelijk kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.moreInfo: Municipalities arrange care for residents in some cases, such as home care and youth care. On the one hand, the municipality is obliged to take the resident's personal situation into account. On the other hand, residents cannot force the municipality to provide a care provider with a certain religion or belief. The municipality chooses which care provider it contracts with. This can have consequences for the options available to the resident.
statements.pro: Some parties say good coordination reduces the chance of misunderstandings. Misunderstandings can lead to a wrong diagnosis or poorer treatment. Good coordination leads to better control of healthcare costs.
statements.con: Some parties point out that greater freedom of choice leads to higher costs. More choice goes against the principle that care should be equal for everyone.

statements.title: Entrepreneurs should be able to apply for debt counseling while keeping their business.

titleParts.glossary: The municipality helps people get out of debt. They receive an amount to live on and the rest of their income goes to their creditors.

titleParts.text: kunnen aanvragen, terwijl hun bedrijf behouden blijft.

statements.moreInfo: Entrepreneurs with financial problems in the municipality of Helmond can turn to Senzer. This organization helps them free of charge with debt restructuring, refinancing and business coaching.
statements.pro: Some parties believe this prevents bankruptcy and preserves jobs.
statements.con: Some parties state that the municipality thereby helps residents who are not able to run a business.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.moreInfo: People who have no work and no other benefit can apply to the municipality for a social assistance benefit. The municipality may demand a counter-performance for this. They then do socially useful work for which they are not paid.
statements.pro: Some parties say it keeps people in contact with others and thus involved in society. This provides structure to their days and helps them find paid work.
statements.con: Several parties say that tailored solutions are more effective. Helping people with individualized support into work works better than making it mandatory. There are too many differences among people receiving social assistance to introduce such a general rule.

statements.title: The municipality should participate in the celebration of Pride Week by the LGBTQIA+ community.
statements.moreInfo: "Pride" is English for "pride." Pride week is a period with worldwide attention to the visibility and rights of the LGBTQIA+ community. LGBTQIA+ stands for lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer, intersex, asexual and (+) all other forms of gender identity and sexual attraction. Helmond is a Rainbow Municipality and works to promote acceptance and safety for the LGBTQIA+ community. The municipality shows this, among other things, by flying the rainbow flag on Coming Out Day and by the Inclusion Agenda.
statements.pro: Some parties think the municipality thereby communicates that everyone is equal. This shows the municipality is against discrimination.
statements.con: Some parties think participation goes too far. They believe the municipality should facilitate the celebration but not take part itself. Promoting equality should be evident from municipal policy, not from participating in Pride Week.

statements.title: The municipality should not use cameras in public spaces that automatically recognize faces.

titleParts.glossary: Outdoor areas that are accessible to everyone, such as streets, squares, and parks.

titleParts.text: geen camera’s gebruiken die automatisch gezichten herkennen.

statements.moreInfo: Cameras that automatically recognize faces are in principle prohibited. They infringe on people's privacy. There are very few exceptions, and these are only possible under conditions and if the law expressly permits them.
statements.pro: Several parties point out that the use of facial recognition infringes on citizens' privacy. The use of cameras with facial recognition in public spaces is in principle prohibited.
statements.con: Some parties believe that cameras with facial recognition can contribute to safety. A general ban on these cameras is too restrictive. There should remain room to use them.

statements.title: Helmond should stop waiving municipal taxes for residents with low incomes.

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.moreInfo: Some low-income residents in Helmond do not have to pay (part of) the municipal taxes. This is called exemption. It concerns sewer charges, property tax (the tax for an owned home) and waste collection charges. Residents can apply for this. The municipality looks not only at income but also at assets. Thus exemption is not possible for residents with an expensive car or a lot of money in the bank. If the municipality abolishes the exemption, tax revenue will increase.
statements.pro: According to some parties, the costs of write-offs are borne by other residents. Taxes for all residents of Helmond should be lowered, not only for a limited group. Help should only go to people who really need it.
statements.con: According to several parties, residents will get into financial trouble if waivers are abolished. Abolishing waivers leads to more inequality. Abolishment leads to stress and poor health and, in the long run, to higher costs.

statements.title: The municipality should pay for music lessons for children from low-income families.
statements.moreInfo: The costs for music lessons amount to hundreds of euros per year. For families with a low income this can be a barrier, preventing children from taking music lessons. Stichting Leergeld is active in many municipalities, including Helmond. This foundation pays the costs for children from these families. To do its work the foundation receives donations from individuals and companies. But the largest part is paid by the municipality of Helmond.
statements.pro: Some parties believe music education for children is an investment in lifelong development. It is unfair if cultural development depends on parents' income. Music lessons contribute to self-confidence.
statements.con: According to some parties, there is already a scheme through Stichting Leergeld. That is sufficient. Music lessons are already provided at school. It would cost a lot of money if the municipality had to pay for this.

statements.title: The municipality should communicate with its residents only in Dutch.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality communicates in, for example, letters or information to residents in Dutch. However, Helmond has a large group of residents whose mother tongue is not Dutch. If important or necessary, the message is sometimes also communicated in another language. In addition, the municipality's website has a translate button. This makes it possible to translate the page into a language of choice.
statements.pro: Some parties think communicating in Dutch is good for integration. By communicating in Dutch the municipality places the responsibility to understand information on the resident. Residents of Helmond should be able to read and speak Dutch.
statements.con: According to some parties, the municipality is less accessible if it only communicates in Dutch. By offering information in more languages, the municipality ensures equal opportunities. Providing information in multiple languages makes municipal services work better.

statements.title: There should be a housing team that supports tenants with complaints about their home or landlord.
statements.moreInfo: Tenants in Helmond may face problems such as overdue maintenance, high rents or conflicts with the landlord. A tenancy team can help with advice, support and contact with the landlord. The Housing Quality Act gives the municipality a number of related tasks. For example, the municipality must set up a reporting point for tenants, supervise landlords and can determine when landlords must have a rental permit.
statements.pro: According to some parties, a tenants' team improves the quality of housing. A tenants' team creates a fairer balance between tenant and landlord. A tenants' team offers protection against excessive rents.
statements.con: There are parties who believe it is not the municipality's task to support tenants. Creating a tenant team costs money. It is the landlords' responsibility to handle complaints.

statements.title: Helmond should not be climate-neutral by 2035, but only by 2050.

titleParts.glossary: A situation in which the municipality as a whole does not make climate change worse, for example by limiting CO2 emissions.

statements.moreInfo: The municipality of Helmond wants to be climate-neutral by 2035. This is achieved, for example, by using more solar and wind energy, by heating more homes with district heating or heat pumps, and by planting trees in the city.
statements.pro: Some parties find 2035 not a realistic goal. They consider it unachievable and unaffordable. 2050 aligns better with national and European agreements to become climate neutral. 2050 can count on broader support.
statements.con: According to some parties, speed is of great importance for the climate and for devising solutions. Climate change is a major threat. Helmond will experience more heat and heavy downpours. Helmond must do its part to become climate neutral.

statements.title: In the renovation of the Kasteel-Traverse there should be fewer lanes and fewer turn-offs to side streets.
statements.moreInfo: There are plans to stop through-traffic on the Kasteel-Traverse and to narrow it. This adjustment makes it possible to make a number of side streets off the Kasteel-Traverse car-reduced. Before the change is made the municipality wants to build a bypass for through-traffic.
statements.pro: Some parties think traffic will flow better if there are fewer exits. If there are fewer exits, there are fewer cars in the city center. If there are fewer lanes, there is more space for bike paths and for trees and shrubs.
statements.con: Some parties think that fewer lanes and exits will cause rat-running. Fewer lanes lead to traffic jams, reduced flow, and longer travel times. The Kasteel-Traverse is an important lifeline for Helmond.

statements.title: Residents of Helmond should be able to call enforcement officers (BOAs) directly every day of the week.

titleParts.text: Inwoners van Helmond moeten alle dagen van de week rechtstreeks met

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.
Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

statements.moreInfo: The enforcement officers (boa's) in the municipality of Helmond can currently be reached during office hours via the municipality's central phone number. Outside office hours they can be reached via the police's 0900 number. There is currently no direct telephone line to the boa's.
statements.pro: Some parties believe direct contact with enforcement officers increases safety. Direct contact boosts residents' confidence in enforcement.
statements.con: Some parties find that direct contact by residents with municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) is an intrusion on the officers' privacy. BOAs do not have a welfare role. For that, residents should call the police.

statements.title: The speed limit within the built-up area of Helmond should be 30 km/h, except for a few main roads.

titleParts.glossary: An area with buildings (houses, other structures).
The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.

titleParts.text: 30 km/u worden, met uitzondering van enkele doorgaande wegen.

statements.moreInfo: The maximum speed limit in many places in Helmond is currently still 50 km/h.
statements.pro: According to some parties this will increase traffic safety. A lower maximum speed reduces traffic noise. A lower maximum speed is good for livability.
statements.con: Some parties believe that tailored solutions are better than a general standard. Each street should be assessed to determine whether 30 km/h is an appropriate maximum speed. In many parts of Helmond, 30 km/h is already the speed limit. A lower maximum speed leads to delays.

statements.title: When allocating social rental housing, priority should be given to people who already live, work, or study in Helmond.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: en moeten mensen die al in Helmond wonen, werken of studeren voorrang krijgen.

statements.moreInfo: Municipalities can lay down a so-called housing ordinance. In it they can include that housing seekers with a local connection get priority. This priority applies to a maximum of 50% of the available rental homes. The Helmond housing corporations are connected to Wooniezie. This is a regional platform with rental housing offers. The municipal council has adopted a housing ordinance. This ordinance mainly regulates urgency and does not contain a general priority scheme for regular housing seekers based on living, working or studying in Helmond.
statements.pro: Some parties say priority for home-seekers with local ties is good because it increases social cohesion in neighborhoods. Many residents of Helmond have been waiting a long time for a social rental home. Helmond residents, through their work and their contributions to associations, make Helmond what it is.
statements.con: Some parties state that house seekers with a local connection already receive most of the allocations. That makes this rule redundant. Giving priority to applicants with a local connection is not permitted by law and leads to discrimination. Priority for one group results in longer waiting times for the other group.

statements.title: The municipality should pay for public transport within Helmond for low-income residents.
statements.moreInfo: Residents with a low income can in some municipalities travel by bus and train for free or at a discount. In Amsterdam a number of routes are free for people on minimum income. The city of Groningen has free city and regional transport for residents with a minimum income. In a number of Limburg municipalities people with an income at the social minimum can travel for free outside rush hour on buses and trains operated by Arriva.
statements.pro: According to some parties, free public transport makes low-income residents more mobile. Free public transport provides better access to healthcare and education. Free public transport for low-income residents increases the chance of finding work.
statements.con: According to several parties, it is too expensive for the municipality to offer free public transport to low-income residents. The province, not the municipality, is responsible for public transport. There are already other ways to travel cheaply by public transport.

statements.title: Only the national rules should apply to the construction of new homes; the municipality should not impose any additional regulations.
statements.moreInfo: National rules apply to the construction of homes. These contain requirements about, among other things, safety, environmental impact and energy use. A municipality may add additional requirements. These can, for example, be about planting more trees in new developments, about energy use or about the appearance of buildings. Helmond also imposes additional requirements in some cases.
statements.pro: Some parties think additional municipal regulations make building homes more expensive. Extra municipal rules lead to delays in construction. Extra municipal rules lead to more bureaucracy.
statements.con: Some parties believe that extra municipal regulations allow for tailored solutions. The municipality knows exactly what is needed for a particular location. The municipality has the authority to impose additional rules and should be able to use that authority. Additional rules are not always harmful; they can also ensure faster construction.

statements.title: Work the municipality outsources should, as much as possible, be done by companies with employees on permanent contracts.
statements.moreInfo: Giving priority to companies that have employees on permanent contracts falls under "socially responsible procurement." The Dutch government wants all public authorities to make efforts in this area. When a municipality outsources work, it must comply with European procurement rules. The municipality will, for example, have to explain why working with employees on permanent contracts is important for the contract. It must also indicate how it will assess whether employees are on permanent contracts.
statements.pro: Some parties believe it is better if employees have permanent contracts. Employees then have greater social security. Companies with permanent staff provide higher quality. Companies with permanent staff provide more continuity.
statements.con: Some parties believe that the company that delivers the best work should get the contract. It does not matter what type of employment contracts the workers have. Giving contracts as much as possible to companies with permanently employed staff is more expensive. Awarding contracts primarily to companies with permanent employees leads to less choice.

statements.title: Employers should provide housing for seasonal workers themselves, preferably on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

titleParts.text: , indien mogelijk op hun eigen terrein.

statements.moreInfo: Seasonal workers work temporarily at companies, for example in agriculture or logistics. In Helmond their housing is currently sometimes arranged through landlords or by the municipality. If employers arrange housing themselves, possibly on their own site, this reduces pressure on the housing market. It also means that companies that work with seasonal workers take on extra responsibility.
statements.pro: According to some parties this will prevent even more people from looking for a home in Helmond. The measure prevents extra pressure on the housing market.
statements.con: Some parties point out that if a seasonal worker loses their job, they immediately lose their housing as well.

statements.title: No new solar panels should be installed in natural areas or on agricultural land in Helmond.
statements.moreInfo: In Helmond solar panels are installed on roofs, but sometimes also in nature or on agricultural land. This contributes to generating sustainable energy, but also changes the use and appearance of the landscape. When placing solar panels on agricultural land or in nature the municipality must weigh these different interests.
statements.pro: Some parties believe this preserves space for nature and agriculture.
statements.con: Several parties find that fields of solar panels in nature areas or on agricultural land are important for the transition to sustainable energy.

statements.title: A neighborhood help desk for financial problems should be established in the poorest districts of Helmond.
statements.moreInfo: A financial neighborhood desk is a place where residents of a neighborhood can go if they have money problems. At the desk there is an official or helper who can help residents immediately. If that is not possible, they can put the resident in contact with a specialist official or helper who has the right knowledge. At the moment Social Teams Helmond, together with social partners, organize walk-in consultation hours. These take place in the neighborhood centers of Helmond-North, Helmond-East, Helmond-West and the city center.
statements.pro: Some parties think help works best when offered close to residents. Prevention is better than cure. By tackling problems as early as possible, the municipality prevents them from growing and becoming much harder to solve.
statements.con: Some parties believe there are already other places where this help can be offered. Setting up new neighborhood desks is expensive.

statements.title: Helmond should set up a housing corporation with Eindhoven for mid-rent housing.

titleParts.text: Helmond moet samen met Eindhoven een

titleParts.glossary: A non-profit organization focused on building, managing, and renting affordable housing.

statements.moreInfo: Housing corporations are organizations that build, manage and rent homes. They mainly rent social rental homes. These are homes for people who earn no more than €51,537 gross (single-person household) or €56,910 (multi-person household). The rent is then a maximum of €933 per month. A housing corporation for mid-rent would build homes with a monthly rent of €933 to €1,229.
statements.pro: Some parties say this provides affordable rental homes. There are many people who earn too much for social housing and too little to rent in the private sector or to buy a home. Eindhoven and Helmond already cooperate in other areas. This fits well with that.
statements.con: Some parties think that Eindhoven and Helmond can already cooperate with housing associations on mid-range rents. A new housing corporation is not necessary. Helmond should not cooperate with Eindhoven. Helmond can ensure mid-range rents on its own.

statements.title: Helmond should allow the opening of a second coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: Helmond moet de komst van een tweede

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.moreInfo: Helmond currently has one coffeeshop. In 2021 the municipality commissioned research into whether there is room for additional coffeeshops. That research concluded that one to two more coffeeshops could be added in Helmond. In Helmond the mayor sets the coffeeshop policy, including the maximum number of coffeeshops. The municipal council has influence through the frameworks (such as the General Local Ordinance and spatial rules for locations).
statements.pro: Some parties think a second coffeeshop will eliminate the nuisance caused by illegal street sales of hash and weed. The demand for weed and hash is so large that there is a need for a second coffeeshop.
statements.con: Some parties believe that a second coffeeshop would pose safety risks. There is currently no need for a second coffeeshop.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should pay membership fees and expenses for residents who receive less than 120% of the social minimum. Currently that is 130%.
Residents who want to generate renewable energy together should be able to receive financial support from the municipality.
By removing local rules, the municipality should make it easier to organize events.
New homes should be built in a nature-inclusive way.
There should be a ban on the use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves in Helmond.
The municipality should mow public grass more often.
The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.
The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
The municipality should introduce a vacancy tax on homes that have been empty for more than a year.
People receiving welfare benefits who provide informal care to a family member or acquaintance do not have to apply for jobs.
The municipality should do more to bring attention to the history of slavery. The mayor's apologies were only a first step.
The municipality should, together with schools and companies, ensure that MBO vocational students can do internships.
At least 35% of the new homes to be built in Helmond should be social rental housing.
There should be more meeting places and activities for young people.
The municipality should explain how algorithms are used for benefits, allowances and minimum-income policy.
In new development plans with more than one hundred homes, multi-generational homes should also be built.
Helmond should accommodate more asylum seekers than is legally required.
Seniors who leave a large family home should be able to receive a moving allowance.
More charging points for electric cars should be installed in every neighborhood.
